entries.mem0ai/mem0: A universal memory layer providing multi-level retention for users, sessions, and autonomous agents. It utilizes hybrid retrieval and temporal reasoning to rank memories by date and state.
entries.volcengine/OpenViking: A context database that treats memory and skills as a file system using viking:// URIs. It employs tiered L0/L1/L2 loading to significantly reduce token costs and improve task completion.
entries.supermemoryai/supermemory: A high-performance memory engine featuring sub-300ms recall and byte-level deduplication for prompt-cache discounts. It includes native connectors for Google Drive, Notion, GitHub, and Gmail.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: An opinionated brain for Hermes agents that uses a self-wiring knowledge graph to extract entity relationships without LLM calls. It includes a synthesis layer for cited answers and over 30 MCP tools.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: A learning-centric memory system implementing retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term knowledge. It offers a simple LLM wrapper with enterprise storage support for PostgreSQL and Oracle AI Database.
entries.campfirein/byterover-cli: A memory provider that treats context as a git repository, enabling branching, merging, and versioning of markdown trees. It features a fast 5-tier retrieval system with sub-100ms latency.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: A stateful agent library that extracts reasoning-based conclusions rather than relying on simple vector matching. It maintains evolving representations of users and projects in prompt-ready formats.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: A recursive self-improvement harness that uses five specialized agent roles to generate structured playbooks. It ensures persistent knowledge transfer via CLI-first skill exports.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: A zero-dependency, SQLite-backed memory system designed for sub-millisecond performance. It combines vector similarity, FTS5, and importance scoring with native MCP support.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A lossless context management plugin using a hierarchical DAG to compact conversation history without losing detail. It allows agents to grep and expand historical nodes via a local SQLite database.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: A self-hosted backend for Honcho that enables private, cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent. It supports Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis with compatibility for any OpenAI-style LLM.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: A local-first context engine that eliminates cloud dependencies and API keys. It employs a hybrid retrieval approach combining BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs.
entries.Signet-AI/signetai: A portable identity and memory layer using SQLite and markdown for local data ownership. It provides cross-harness continuity across Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and OpenClaw.
entries.plur-ai/plur: A shared memory layer using an open YAML engram format for multi-agent systems. It utilizes ACT-R activation to strengthen relevant facts and decay stale information over time.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: A context optimization tool that increases cache hits and reduces prefill latency through prefix reordering and deduplication. It integrates natively with SGLang, vLLM, and the Hermes ecosystem.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: A reflective memory system featuring ranked graph traversal and BM25 full-text search. It provides automated indexing for local directories, URLs, and multimedia files via edge tags.
entries.yepyhun/Brainstack: A memory kernel that consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval. It uses a multi-layered storage stack comprising SQLite, Kuzu, and Chroma.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: An anticipatory memory system that prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs. It is a local-first MCP server utilizing SQLite-vec and GGUF models.
entries.yantrikos/yantrikdb-hermes-plugin: A self-maintaining plugin that provides explainable recall via why_retrieved tags. It automatically handles canonicalization, contradiction tracking, and recency ranking.
entries.Ladybug-Memory/hermes-memory-plugin: A local-only plugin using a columnar .lbdb embedded graph database. It employs importance-weighted recall and BM25 search to surface high-priority memories first.
entries.alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer: A utility that reduces tool-schema overhead using BM25 ranking for keyword-based tool selection. It includes a dashboard to track prompt token savings for Hermes and MCP tools.
entries.MukundaKatta/hermes-agentmemory: An episodic memory plugin featuring a pull-model for retrieval and true deletion of memories. It maintains a trace.jsonl audit log for every memory operation.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major industry frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It enables Hermes agents to perform sub-second discovery and execution across 26 security domains using the agentskills.io standard.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows agents to generate complex draw.io diagrams, including UML and deep learning architectures, from natural language descriptions. It features a visual feedback loop that automatically detects and fixes layout issues before exporting to formats like PNG or SVG.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: Designed to eliminate detectable AI patterns, this tool uses a two-pass audit and a 42-category pattern library to rewrite content. It helps Hermes agents produce more human-like text by utilizing tiered vocabulary tables and specialized detection modes.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw introduces an 'Agentic Evolver' that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops. It unifies skill libraries across multiple devices and teams, offering native integration with Hermes and OpenAI-compatible APIs.
entries.wondelai/skills: This is a modular, cross-platform library providing specialized frameworks for architecture, UX design, and product strategy. It is fully compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io, allowing for easy installation via a dedicated CLI.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose reusable skill patterns. It streamlines development by generating both SKILL.md documentation and Python scaffolds directly from observed agent actions.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI with a focus on type safety. It utilizes progressive disclosure of metadata to significantly reduce token usage during skill discovery.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub serves as a community marketplace for browsing and installing skills, featuring automated security scanning with over 65 threat detection rules. It includes an agent-to-agent feedback system and supports monetization through crypto and Stripe payments.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: This project teaches Hermes agents to write their own analytical prompts and identify structural conservation laws within complex codebases. It generates constraint reports that highlight potential analysis blind spots and limitations in the agent's reasoning.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a harness for long-running agents that manages shared state locally without requiring a database. It decomposes complex specifications into multi-task execution plans and uses JSON handoff envelopes for seamless transitions between sessions.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming skill enables agents to weave prose, diagrams, and source code into unified .lit.md files. It includes a tangler to extract runnable code and automatically syncs edits between source files and their documentation.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides a shared memory system via markdown-based fabric directories, allowing agents to remember past work across different instances. It features an end-to-end pipeline for extracting decision-quality data to fine-tune replacement models.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library provides standardized skills for interacting with Chainlink oracles, including CCIP, VRF, and Data Feeds. It follows the agentskills.io spec to facilitate secure smart contract onboarding and local workflow simulations.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill Forge converts existing code repositories and developer documentation into verified, agentskills.io-compliant instructions. It ensures high reliability by providing direct traceability back to source file line numbers.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: This official repository provides standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for the FLUX image generation model family. It enables Hermes to handle precise hex color specifications and structured JSON prompting for high-fidelity visual outputs.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: SkillDock is a registry for reusable AI skills that supports automated installation with recursive dependency resolution. It features a Python SDK for discovery and offers monetization options via Stripe and the TON blockchain.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill enables autonomous execution of multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints for real-time progress tracking. It ensures operational safety by providing rollback procedures and recovery mechanisms for long-running agent processes.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading architecture to reduce token usage by up to 86%. It maintains persistent server connections via a socket-based daemon and supports hot-reloading of configurations.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This C# and .NET implementation of the agentskills.io specification brings tool-based skill loading to the Microsoft.Extensions.AI ecosystem. It supports progressive disclosure via SKILL.md files and automatic registration of common skill directories.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill integrates Hermes with Nextcloud, allowing the agent to manage files, calendars, and contacts via WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It ensures secure communication over HTTPS and includes a guided setup script for credential validation.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a comprehensive collection of Nix packages for various AI coding agents, including Hermes. It features automated daily updates and supports both open-source and proprietary binary-distributed agentic tools.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent includes a GUI, 100 built-in tools, and support for local models via LM Studio. It functions without Docker, offering a dynamic tool maker and workflow engine for custom automation.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: This solution focuses on high-security environments by sandboxing Hermes Agent with hardware-level enforcement via NVIDIA OpenShell. It offers three security policy presets and a pre-built skills library for tasks like research digests and code reviews.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project offers a streamlined Docker sandbox image that includes the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It supports persistent state through mounted home directories and allows for specific versioning via build arguments.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative and reproducible deployments of Hermes, managing configurations, documents, and systemd services. It ensures secure secrets handling through sops-nix integration and supports hybrid skill management.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeted at production environments, this project provides a headless systemd deployment for Ubuntu and Debian servers. It configures Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe service to enable the autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ acts as a Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple Hermes Agent instances through a single web application. It enables hierarchy-aware inter-agent delegation, task scheduling, and enterprise MCP access.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment method uses a Docker Compose stack integrated directly with Portainer as a Git Repository stack. It features a ttyd web terminal interface and automates image builds using GitHub Actions.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows using a local SQLite database with zero external dependencies. It serves as a centralized hub for dispatching tasks across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen while providing a human-in-the-loop review system for task sign-off.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Designed for building autonomous swarms, this framework features a visual protocol builder for designing structured agent delegations and durable memory. It supports over 23 LLM providers, making it a versatile choice for coordinating Hermes alongside other local or cloud-based models.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This system coordinates 17 specialized agents across functional domains such as research, infrastructure, and quality assurance. It automates complex task routing through predefined pipelines that enforce mandatory security and quality gates for every code change.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This framework focuses on meta-optimization by using an automated outer-loop search to refine agent harness code. It orchestrates Hermes through structured candidate mutations to track and improve performance frontiers on benchmarks like TBLite.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes, vie for GitHub stars. The platform orchestrates nine distinct agents and provides a real-time dashboard to visualize task execution, code generation, and scoreboard metrics.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This project implements ACP-style delegation to route tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing context pollution. It emphasizes safety through isolated workflows, configurable timeouts, and strict output character limits.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron executes an eight-phase autonomous software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It ensures architectural integrity by requiring automated graph-diff gates to be passed at every stage of the development process.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based CLI tool provides a high-performance TUI and 3D dashboard for monitoring token consumption and costs across various AI coding agents. It is essential for Hermes developers who need to track usage metrics and budget overhead across multiple model integrations simultaneously.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal experience through YAML-based themes, custom ASCII banners, and unique spinner animations. It belongs in this list as the primary visual customization engine for developers who want to share or modify the aesthetic identity of their agent interface.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and its 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel, enabling native execution without external processes or OAuth requirements. It is a specialized tool for developers who require MLOps capabilities, such as vLLM inference and RL training, within a unified environment.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: Vessel is an AI-native browser that exposes page visibility and control to autonomous agents via MCP endpoints while providing a UI for human supervision. It serves as a critical bridge for Hermes agents that need to navigate the web with durable session state and action rollback capabilities.
entries.abruptive/Ankh.md: Ankh.md introduces a per-folder scoping framework that allows developers to create specialized Hermes agents with project-local memories, tools, and instructions. It is ideal for managing complex workflows where the agent must automatically switch configurations based on the current working directory.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static linter uses seven deterministic detector patterns to analyze agent configurations and system prompts without making expensive LLM calls. It provides a cost-effective way to enforce structural quality and HERM v1.1 scoring standards within offline CI/CD pipelines.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment script automates the setup of a full Hermes stack including LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant using Docker for zero-cost local inference. It is the go-to resource for quickly bootstrapping an environment pre-configured with 29 community plugins and optimized model routing.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: Designed specifically for ecosystem migration, this tool automates the transfer of personas, skills, and workspace files from OpenClaw to the Hermes YAML format. It ensures a seamless transition for developers by mapping complex model configurations and local credentials to the Hermes architecture.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This lightweight, framework-free interface provides a classic three-panel layout for managing sessions, chat, and workspace files simultaneously. It maintains full parity with the Hermes CLI, offering a seamless transition for users who want a web-based or mobile-friendly experience without complex installation steps.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: A comprehensive desktop companion that simplifies the setup process through guided installation for both local and remote backends. It distinguishes itself by managing 16 different messaging gateways and providing specialized tools for memory editing and task scheduling.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: This analytics-focused dashboard offers unified configuration for eight messaging platforms and detailed tracking of token consumption and costs. It features real-time chat streaming with markdown support and a dedicated inspector for monitoring agent tool calls.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A native web workspace that integrates a terminal, memory browser, and skill manager into a single interface. It is unique for its 'Swarm Mode,' which allows users to orchestrate multiple persistent, role-based autonomous agents within a vanilla Hermes environment.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: Tailored specifically for macOS, this workspace uses native SSH and aliases to connect directly to Hermes hosts. It manages remote sessions and cron jobs alongside Kanban boards, supporting complex multi-agent workflows through selectable profiles.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: This self-hosted gateway acts as a central hub for managing multiple Hermes profiles and monitoring system metrics. It provides a robust terminal and file explorer behind a secure login, complemented by detailed analytics on model usage and platform costs.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React application designed to run as a Telegram Mini App, featuring a terminal-style chat interface with slash command support. It allows users to spawn background agents and monitor live outputs or manage scheduled cron jobs directly from their mobile device.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: This glassmorphic web interface uses a lightweight Python proxy to deliver a single-file React application for managing self-hosted assistants. It combines real-time log streaming with an integrated file browser and management tools for MCP skills and cron jobs.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows desktop solution that operates without admin rights or Docker, making Hermes highly accessible. It comes pre-loaded with over 100 tools, including ComfyUI and TTS, and features a dynamic tool maker for creating custom API wrappers.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: Focused on process monitoring, this dashboard provides real-time visibility into gateway status and session activity. It features deep conversation inspection using FTS5 search, allowing users to audit message-level details and track costs across the ecosystem.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace that emphasizes extensibility through an integrated marketplace for discovering skills and MCP servers. It offers isolated profile management to keep sessions, memory, and API keys separate while providing granular runtime controls for tool approvals.
